subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

files.fileName: IA de Mate - Nicole Fallas AguÌero (6).pdf

strengths: Headings, tables and figures provide an identifiable structure
The final regression equation (y = 0.877x + 21.6) is concise and well presented, supporting a clear conclusion
weaknesses: Transitions are abrupt with lengthy digressions on physiology before data are shown
Figure numbering resets and page labelling is inconsistent
Repetition of background material and concluding aphorism detract from conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows adequate organization and a clear introduction-analysis-conclusion sequence, but uneven transitions, inconsistent labelling and unnecessary repetition prevent it from reaching band 3. The structure is present and coherent in parts, fitting band 2 for Presentation.

strengths: Use of standard notation for mean, standard deviation and correlation
Inclusion of a data table and a scatter plot with line of best fit
Effective use of Figure 2 to classify |r| and interpret correlation strength
weaknesses: Inconsistent subscripts (e.g. Sâ vs S_Î³) and mis-typed formulas
Incorrect intercept formula and misplaced variables in definitions
Lack of residual or histogram analysis to verify model assumptions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are employed, and there are two representations (table, scatter plot), but mis-typed formulas, inconsistent subscripts and missing model-assumption checks limit the work to band 2 for Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Clear personal connection to mental-health experiences drives the exploration
Well-described sampling protocol with environmental controls
Proposal to test a depressive memory shows initiative and creativity
weaknesses: Independent thinking is limited, following textbook procedures
Mathematical approach lacks novel adaptation beyond standard correlation/regression
Creative extensions are mentioned but not fully developed in the main analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the studentâs personal approach is evident and sampling design shows initiative, independent thinking and sustained creativity are moderate rather than outstanding. This aligns with band 2 for Personal Engagement.

strengths: Discussion of rÂ² and residual variance demonstrates some critical reflection
weaknesses: Reflection on standard error is brief and largely descriptive
Model-assumption critique (linearity, homoscedasticity, independence) is absent
Connections to why the model works are superficial
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but remains at a basic levelâthere is limited depth, critique and connection to understandingâso the work fits band 1 for Reflection.

strengths: Applies relevant SL AI syllabus mathematics (Pearson correlation, standard deviation, regression)
Comprehensive data table supports the statistical analysis
Procedures for correlation and regression are correctly identified
weaknesses: Intercept formula is mis-written and rounding is inconsistent
Several intermediate steps are omitted, and assumptions are not addressed
Partial correctness of calculations limits overall accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is appropriate at course level, but errors in formulas, omitted steps and partial accuracy constrain the work to band 2 for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a familiar, personally relevant theme shows some individuality, but the mathematical approach (basic correlation and regression) closely follows textbook procedure with little novel adaptation.  Evidence of independent thinking is therefore limited.

The student shows understanding of basic statistical concepts (r, rÂ², standard deviation) yet explanations occasionally confuse symbols and do not address assumptions such as linearity or independence, indicating "some understanding" rather than good or thorough.

Most numerical values are plausible, but the intercept formula is mis-written and rounding is inconsistent; several intermediate sums are omitted, so accuracy is only partial.  Band 2 (âpartially correctâ) matches this performance.

Much of the text repeats the same claim of a âstrong positive correlationâ and lengthy background paragraphs on hormones add little to the statistical objective.  Nevertheless, the majority of material is connected to the investigation, so the work is "mostly relevant" but not consistently concise.

The student employs standard notation for mean (\overline{X}), standard deviation (S_x) and correlation (r).  However subscripts are mis-matched (S_Î³ defined twice), variables are sometimes typed as plain text, and the regression-intercept formula is given incorrectly, indicating only "some appropriate" mathematical language.

Explanations generally match the working, yet occasional inconsistencies (e.g. calling m both slope and âpendienteâ while later writing Y_m) cause confusion.  Overall clarity is acceptable but not fully consistent, so band 2 is most fitting.

Pearson correlation, standard deviation, linear regression and standard error are all within the SL AI syllabus and are relevant to the question.  They are applied in a straightforward manner, satisfying the descriptor of "relevant mathematics at course level used".

Two representations are used: a full data table and a scatter plot with a line of best fit.  No further diagrams (boxplots, residual plot, histogram) are offered, so the work demonstrates "some" but not "effective" use of multiple representations.
Reflection is confined to a brief restatement of results and a short note on participants speaking quickly; depth is superficial, aligning with "limited reflection present" in band 1.
Apart from collecting primary data, no especially original mathematical strategy or creative extension is attempted.  Hence only "some" initiative is visible.

The exploration follows a basic introduction-analysis-conclusion sequence and ideas generally build on one another, but transitions are sometimes abrupt and there is occasional back-tracking (e.g. long digressions on physiology before any data are shown).  This fits the descriptor of "some coherence and logical development" rather than the clearer, smoother flow expected for band 3.

Critical discussion of method is minimal; the student does not question calculation errors (e.g. wrong intercept formula) or consider sample-size limitations statistically, showing only basic analysis.

Connections to mathematical understanding are mostly declarative ("this shows a strong positive correlation") without exploring why the model works or its assumptions, giving merely a limited link to understanding.

Headings, tables and figures provide an identifiable structure, yet pagination and labelling are inconsistent (figure numbers reset, duplicated page numbers).  While the standard sections are present, internal ordering (e.g. conclusions appearing mid-analysis) shows only adequate, not "well-organised" structure.

The student justifies the topic through personal mental-health experiences and designs the data-collection protocol, demonstrating a clear personal approach that guides the work, meeting the band-2 descriptor.

annotations.comment: The concluding aphorism on joy, while stylistic, introduces unnecessary repetition and detracts from the paperâs academic tone.
The student establishes a personal connection to mental health, which effectively drives the exploration and demonstrates a clear personal approach.
Background material on psychology and physiology precedes any mathematical data, causing abrupt transitions and limited logical development.
The introduction states the variables clearly, but it would benefit from justifying why age and pulse difference best address the research question.
Lengthy discussion of adrenaline and fight-or-flight before outlining methodology disrupts coherence; consider moving physiology to an appendix or summary.
Sampling protocol is clearly described with environmental controls, demonstrating initiative and personal engagement in data collection.
Hypothesis Hâ/Háµ¢ are stated but lack precise definitions of variables; specify X and Y explicitly alongside symbols.
The data table is comprehensive, recording sex, age and pulse values systematically, which supports the statistical analysis.
Figure numbering resets and the table footnote duplicates page numbers, indicating inconsistent labelling and structure.
The narrative interprets female resting pulse difference without statistical support; consider conducting a t-test or separate correlation by gender.
Interpretation of Figure 1 lacks residual or histogram analysis to verify linear model assumptions and data distribution.
The Pearson formula is mis-typed: denominator uses Y instead of \overline{Y}. Subscripts are inconsistent, causing potential misinterpretation.
Expanding the full summation in one table is hard to follow; summarise partial sums before substitution to enhance clarity.
The student calculates r = 749.75 / 850.337558 but does not report the final value to an appropriate precision (e.g., r â 0.881).
Use of Figure 2 to classify |r| is effective, showing clear multiple representations to interpret correlation strength.
Subscripts S_{Î³} and S_{v} are introduced inconsistently; adopt a single subscript convention to avoid confusion.
The standard deviation calculation is presented in full, but the large expression obscures the result; summarise the numerator sum before applying the square root.
Notation switches between s_x and S_x; maintain consistent mathematical notation throughout.
The intercept formula uses b = \frac{1}{y} - m \cdot \frac{1}{x}, which is incorrect; it should be b = \overline{Y} - m\,\overline{X}.
In the list defining S_y, the text mistakenly refers to x instead of y; proofread definitions to ensure accuracy.
The final regression equation y = 0.877x + 21.6 is concise and well presented, supporting a clear conclusion.
The scatter plot lacks a residual plot or goodness-of-fit indicators; consider adding residual analysis for deeper insight.
The formula for the standard error of estimate is stated but the substitution and arithmetic are omitted, reducing clarity.
Reflection on the meaning of the standard error is brief and descriptive; deeper critical evaluation of model limitations would strengthen the reflection.
The discussion of rÂ² and residual variance demonstrates substantial critical reflection on explained versus unexplained variance.
Assumptions of linear regression (linearity, independence, homoscedasticity) are not addressed; consider critically evaluating these in the conclusion.
The proposal to test a depressive memory in future work shows outstanding creativity and initiative for extending the exploration.
